lessons.title: The Digital Paper Trail: What Are Logs?
lessons.description: Discover what logs are, why they exist, and where Linux hides the evidence of your actions.

lessons.title: Finding Yourself: Tracking Your Own Footprints
lessons.description: Use `journalctl`, `last`, and `/var/log` to see exactly what a hacker would see about you.

lessons.title: Cleaning Up: Removing Evidence from Logs
lessons.description: Learn how to selectively delete, shred, and tamper with log files to hide your presence.

lessons.title: The OPSEC Mindset: Thinking Like a Ghost
lessons.description: Beyond tools—develop the operational security mindset to avoid leaving traces in the first place.

lessons.title: Advanced Anti-Forensics and Stealth
lessons.description: Manipulate timestamps, use memory-only execution, and understand the limits of log wiping.
